app_subtitle: Explore recurring conflict cycles and emotional reactions
complete_message: Recognizing your cycle is the first step toward changing it. By pausing in the reactive moments, you create space for connection.

screen1.intro: Ever feel trapped in the same argument?
screen1.title: Conflict cycles are patterns, not personal failures.
screen1.desc: In couples, arguments often run on autopilot. We get triggered, react defensively, and trigger our partner in return. This loop isn't about lack of love; it's a predictable dance of protection.
screen1.quote: "We repeat what we don't resolve — not out of malice, but because the cycle is looking for a new ending."

screen2.title: Anger is often a shield for deeper vulnerabilities.
screen2.desc: When we feel unheard, unimportant, or rejected, our instinct is to protect ourselves by criticizing or shutting down. Underneath the surface, both partners are usually asking: 'Are you there for me?'
screen2.quote: "An emotional trigger is a map showing where our old wounds meet our current relationship."

screen3.title: The Pursuer-Withdrawer Cycle.
screen3.desc: In many couples, one partner pursues (protesting disconnection through criticism or high urgency), while the other withdraws (protecting the bond by shutting down to avoid escalation). The more one pursues, the more the other withdraws.
screen3.quote: "Neither role is the villain. The cycle itself is the enemy you both need to face."

screen4.intro: What are we actually asking for?
screen4.title: Every reaction hides a request for safety.
screen4.desc: When we say 'You never listen,' we are often feeling lonely. When we say 'You're too sensitive,' we are often feeling inadequate. Learning to speak the language of core needs helps defuse the defense systems.
screen4.quote: "Behind every difficult behavior is a vulnerable, unspoken emotional need."

screen5.title: Choose connection over being right.
screen5.desc: You cannot change your partner's reactions, but you can change your own. A single partner stepping out of the cycle and sharing their primary emotion (fear, sadness, loneliness) can shift the entire dynamic.
screen5.quote: "Between trigger and response lies a choice. In that choice lies the potential for healing."
